title: Honey Garlic Cajun Chicken Thighs
imagePrompt: Bone-in chicken thighs sit skin-side up in a pale cream sauce, their skin deeply browned and lacquered to a glossy finish from the honey-butter glaze and oven heat. The Cajun spice crust — dark red from smoked paprika, nearly mahogany at the edges — contrasts sharply with the ivory sauce pooling around and beneath the thighs, lemon slices and smashed garlic cloves softened between the pieces. Shot in the cast iron skillet on a dark charcoal slate surface, single warm light raking from the upper left to catch the sheen of the glaze and the cream sauce's pooling texture. ¾ overhead, skillet handle visible on one side; a small sprig of thyme and a cracked garlic clove beside the pan as earned props.
subtitle: Cast iron-seared bone-in thighs finished in a honey garlic cream sauce.

ingredients.heading: Ingredients
ingredients.note: A Cajun spice blend seared into crispy skin, then finished in a rich cream pan sauce.
items: 4 tbsp olive oil (divided: 2 tbsp for coating, 2 tbsp for the pan)
ingredients.callout: Key tip: Sear skin-side down without moving — that's how you get a crispy, deeply browned crust.

body.html: Bone-in thighs coated in a Cajun spice blend, seared hard in a cast iron for crispy skin, then finished in the oven sitting in a honey garlic Parmesan cream sauce.

items: Sear skin-side down in cast iron 5–7 min
Build sauce from pan drippings — shallot, garlic, wine, cream, Parm

body.html: Serve with: Mashed potatoes and broccolini to soak up the sauce.

slides.title: Season the Chicken
slides.checkboxLabel: Coat chicken in oil and spice blend

items: Mix all spices together in a small bowl.
Coat chicken thighs in 2 tbsp olive oil, then press the spice blend onto all sides.

body.html: Even coverage matters: Press the spices in — don't just dust them on.

items: Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a cast iron skillet over medium-high heat.
Place chicken skin-side down — don't touch it.
Sear 5–7 minutes until the skin is deep golden and releases easily.
If thighs are large, flip and sear the second side 3–5 minutes.

body.html: Don't move it: The skin will stick at first — wait for it to release naturally for the best crust.

slides.title: Build the Sauce
slides.checkboxLabel: Shallot → garlic → wine → cream → Parm → honey

items: In the same skillet over medium heat, sauté shallot 3 minutes until softened.
Add minced garlic and cook until fragrant, about 30–60 seconds.
Deglaze with white wine — scrape up all the browned bits.
Reduce wine 5 minutes, then add chicken broth and simmer briefly.
Stir in heavy cream, then grate in Parmesan.
Add lime juice and honey. Taste and adjust sweetness.

body.html: Balance check: The honey should round out the acidity — add a little at a time and taste as you go.

items: Nestle chicken thighs back into the skillet, skin-side up.
Tuck in lemon slices and 2 smashed garlic cloves.
Continue baking 15–20 minutes more until chicken is cooked through (165°F internal).

body.html: Butter brush timing: Brushing partway through (not at the start) keeps the skin crispy and adds a glossy finish.
